name: Cajun Jambalaya
tagline: Smoked sausage, chicken, and rice in a smoky cajun base
highlight: One-pot Louisiana classic — no soy, all smoke

hero.alt: Cajun jambalaya with sausage, chicken, and rice
hero.caption: jambalaya — Louisiana spice

healthFacts: Andouille sausage is smoky and pork-based — no soy fillers when you buy quality.
The 'holy trinity' (onion, celery, bell pepper) is Cajun mirepoix.

steps.title: Brown the meat
steps.detail: Brown andouille in a Dutch oven 4 min. Set aside. Brown chicken in the rendered fat 6 min. Set aside.
steps.tip: Andouille fat is your seasoning — leave it in the pot.

steps.title: Holy trinity
steps.detail: Add onion, celery, bell pepper to the pot. Cook 8 min until softened. Add garlic and cajun seasoning, cook 1 min.
steps.tip: Cajun seasoning is salt-heavy — don't add more salt yet.

steps.title: Tomato, rice, stock
steps.detail: Stir in tomatoes; cook 3 min. Add rice; toast 2 min. Pour in stock; return meat. Bring to a boil.
steps.tip: Toasting the rice keeps each grain separate.

steps.title: Cover and cook
steps.detail: Cover, drop heat to low, cook 20 min undisturbed. Rest 10 min covered off heat. Fluff with a fork.
steps.tip: Don't peek — rice steaming requires trapped heat.
